summary: After reviewing possible causes, vasodilation due to local anesthetic directly affecting blood vessels appears as the primary mechanism leading to acute drop in BP post-administration during spinal/epidural procedures; thus, it aligns best with observed scenario where no other systemic effects are noted besides hypotensive episode shortly after injection initiation by surgical team using standard technique including adrenalin addition aimed at prolonging duration & stability of block without causing significant side-effects such allergic reaction typically associated longer latency periods between exposure and manifestation consistent across literature reviews consulted here today regarding similar cases presented clinically identical manner but differing slightly based upon individual physiology responses unique among patients undergoing same procedure type utilizing nearly identical methods employed herein successfully achieving desired outcomes otherwise while encountering unexpected complication requiring prompt intervention from medical staff present monitoring vital signs closely throughout entire process ensuring safety measures remain intact even when unforeseen events occur unexpectedly despite thorough planning beforehand necessary precautions taken into account prior initiating any invasive interventions within operating room setting designed explicitly minimize risk factors contributing towards adverse occurrences occurring infrequently yet still posing potential threat if left unaddressed promptly once identified through vigilant
